2605.25402.title: Anatomy-Anchored Self-Supervision: Distilling Vision Foundation Models for Invariant Ultrasound Representation
2605.25402.paper_summary: Self-supervised pre-training paradigm has gained increasing prominence for learning transferable representations in medical imaging, yet existing methods for ultrasound (US) images operate at the image or frame level, overlooking the anatomical context for clinical-aligned representation learning. In this work, we propose an anatomy-anchored ultrasound self-supervision framework ANAUS that shifts representation learning from generic visual regions to clinically meaningful anatomical structures. Utilizing a learnable latent prompt engine alongside a one-time domain adaptation on existing public image--mask pairs, we empower the LP-SAM module to achieve annotation-free anatomy delineation at scale. Building upon this anatomical grounding, we propose a dual-policy self-supervised learning paradigm consisting of inter-view semantics-aware anatomy-separating alignment and contextual core-region prediction to enhance representation learning. Specifically, the former enforces feature invariance within identical anatomical regions while promoting discriminability across distinct structures; the latter compels the model to reconstruct corrupted regions, thereby capturing fine-grained structural details. Extensive evaluations on six public datasets demonstrate that \ours{} consistently outstrips current state-of-the-art methods while maintaining the computational efficiency essential for clinical deployment. Code is available at https://github.com/zhcz328/ANAUS.
2605.25402.paper_summary_zh: 自我監督的預訓練範式在醫學影像中獲得了越來越多的關注，用於學習可轉移的表示，然而現有的超聲（US）影像方法僅在影像或幀級別運作，忽視了臨床對齊表示學習的解剖上下文。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個以解剖為基礎的超聲自我監督框架ANAUS，將表示學習從通用視覺區域轉移到臨床有意義的解剖結構。利用可學習的潛在提示引擎以及對現有公共影像-遮罩對的一次性領域適應，我們使LP-SAM模組能夠在大規模上實現無標註的解剖描繪。在此解剖基礎上，我們提出了一種雙政策自我監督學習範式，包括視角間語義感知的解剖分離對齊和上下文核心區域預測，以增強表示學習。具體而言，前者強制在相同的解剖區域內保持特徵不變性，同時促進不同結構之間的可辨識性；後者迫使模型重建損壞的區域，從而捕捉細緻的結構細節。在六個公共數據集上的廣泛評估顯示，\ours{}始終超越當前的最先進方法，同時保持臨床部署所需的計算效率。代碼可在 https://github.com/zhcz328/ANAUS 獲得。
2605.25402.author: Chunzheng Zhu et.al.
2605.25402.authors: Chunzheng Zhu, Yijun Wang, Jianxin Lin, Feng Wang, Hongwei Wang, Lei Zhao, Shengli Li, Kenli Li
